evals.expected_output: Summary of OpenClaw + World Monitor automated news research video. Links section MUST include https://github.com/koala73/worldmonitor (discussed extensively in transcript as the repo the agent clones and studies). Links MUST also include worldmonitor.app. Links must NOT include creator's social media, Patreon, or Discord links.

assertions.name: includes_github_repo_from_transcript
assertions.description: The GitHub repo is mentioned verbally in the transcript — the skill must extract it even if it only appears in speech, not the description

assertions.name: includes_worldmonitor_app
assertions.description: The project homepage should appear in links

assertions.name: has_links_section
assertions.description: Output must have a Links section at the bottom

assertions.name: no_patreon_links
assertions.description: Promotional links should be filtered out

assertions.name: no_discord_links
assertions.description: Community invite links should be filtered out

assertions.name: has_tldr
assertions.description: Summary must include a TL;DR section

assertions.name: has_key_takeaways
assertions.description: Summary must include key takeaways

evals.expected_output: Research report that visits GitHub repo and worldmonitor.app. Should include repo details (stars, description, language) from actually visiting the link. Must have Projects & tools discussed section with details from the visited pages.

assertions.name: includes_github_repo
assertions.description: Research mode must find and include the GitHub repo

assertions.name: has_projects_section
assertions.description: Research report must have a Projects & tools discussed section

assertions.name: has_research_report_header
assertions.description: Output should be labeled as a Research Report

assertions.name: visited_repo_details
assertions.description: Research mode should show evidence of actually visiting the GitHub repo (stars, language, README details)

evals.prompt: summarize https://youtu.be/YmlqTB7EJy8 what tools does he use?
evals.expected_output: Summary focused on the tools discussed: OpenClaw, Claude Code, World Monitor, Obsidian, GitHub. Links should include relevant project pages. Should answer the specific question about tools while providing a full summary.

assertions.name: mentions_openclaw
assertions.description: Must mention OpenClaw as a key tool

assertions.name: mentions_world_monitor
assertions.description: Must mention World Monitor as a key tool

assertions.name: includes_github_link
assertions.description: Should include at least one GitHub link for the tools discussed

evals.expected_output: Summary of IBM Mixture of Experts podcast covering Perplexity Computer, Claude memory import, NullClaw agent framework, and Tilly Norwood AI actor. Links must be VERIFIED — transcript says 'skill sh' but the correct URL is skills.sh (not skill.sh which is a parked domain). Shell Game podcast URL must resolve (shellgame.co, not theshellgame.org which doesn't exist).

assertions.name: correct_skills_sh_url
assertions.description: Transcript says 'skill sh' but correct URL is skills.sh (plural) — tests that link verification corrects mangled transcript URLs

assertions.name: no_parked_skill_sh
assertions.description: skill.sh (singular) is a parked domain — must not appear as a link. Uses trailing ) to match markdown link format [text](skill.sh)

assertions.name: no_dead_shellgame_org
assertions.description: theshellgame.org does not resolve — link verification must catch this

assertions.name: correct_shellgame_url
assertions.description: The Shell Game podcast lives at shellgame.co — tests that verification finds the correct domain

assertions.name: has_tldr
assertions.description: Summary must include a TL;DR section

assertions.name: has_key_takeaways
assertions.description: Summary must include key takeaways

assertions.name: has_links_section
assertions.description: Output must have a Links section

assertions.name: mentions_nullclaw
assertions.description: Must mention NullClaw as a topic

assertions.name: mentions_perplexity
assertions.description: Must mention Perplexity Computer as a topic
